description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves create a striped pattern, alternating between raised stone and recessed channels, which may serve both functional and aesthetic purposes. The block is solid and robust, with sharp, clean edges and a uniform thickness. Its coloration is primarily a dark gray to black, with lighter gray tones visible in the grooved areas, suggesting a polished or treated stone material. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, emphasizing its geometric simplicity. This type of block is commonly used in urban environments as a barrier, step, or tactile paving element.
description: A rectangular stone block with parallel grooves on its top surface, creating a striped pattern in dark and light gray tones. The block is solid, smooth-sided, and visually robust.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat face and the full width of the parallel grooves.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves across the block.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the striped pattern from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side-angled view, again emphasizing the groove depth and regularity.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a tactile paving or barrier element
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering visible; surfaces appear clean and well-maintained.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: aligned flush with pavement or adjacent blocks
